# TDD Development Approach

Follow strict Test-Driven Development for every subtask:

## Red-Green-Refactor Cycle

1. **Red**: Write a failing test that defines the expected behavior
2. **Green**: Write the minimum code to make the test pass
3. **Refactor**: Clean up the code while keeping all tests green

## Planning Requirements

For each subtask, your plan must specify:
- What test file(s) to create or modify
- What test cases to write (name each test with expected behavior)
- What implementation file(s) to create or modify
- What the implementation should do to pass the tests

## Test Coverage Guidelines

- Cover the happy path for every feature
- Cover edge cases (empty inputs, boundary values, invalid data)
- Cover error handling (what happens when things fail)
- For async operations, test both success and failure paths

## Execution Order

For each subtask, always:
1. Write the test(s) first
2. Run the tests to confirm they fail (red)
3. Implement the feature
4. Run the tests to confirm they pass (green)
5. Refactor if needed, re-run tests

</tdd_instructions>

Create a detailed implementation plan for this task and all its subtasks. Follow the TDD approach described above. Your plan should cover:
1. What tests to write first (for each subtask)
2. What implementation code to write to make the tests pass
3. What files to create or modify
4. Any architectural decisions

Be thorough and specific. This plan will be saved to a file and then executed by an AI agent.

1	# Kanban Board
2	
3	## Problem Statement
4	
5	Team collaboration tools like Jira, Trello, and Linear are powerful but heavyweight: they require account creation, have complex UIs, and introduce friction for small teams who just want to track work. This project provides a dead-simple, self-hosted kanban board accessible via a shared URL — no login, no setup for team members, just open and use.
6	
7	## Target Users
8	
9	Small development or project teams (2–10 people) who:
10	- Work in a trusted environment (same company or project)
11	- Want a visual task board without account management overhead
12	- Need to see each other's changes in real time
13	- Are comfortable with markdown/rich text for task descriptions
14	
15	## Success Metrics
16	
17	- Any team member can create a card, assign it, and move it to "Done" in under 30 seconds
18	- All connected clients reflect any card change within 500ms
19	- Zero accounts required — share a URL and you're in
20	- The app starts with a single `docker compose up` command
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Capability Tree
25	
26	### Capability: Card Management
27	Full lifecycle management of task cards on the board.
28	
29	#### Feature: Create Card
30	- **Description**: Create a new card with a title, optional assignee name, and optional description
31	- **Inputs**: Title (required), assignee (optional free text), column (defaults to "ready")
32	- **Outputs**: Persisted card with unique ID returned to client
33	- **Behavior**: Insert card into DB, assign a position at end of target column, broadcast `card:created` event
34	
35	#### Feature: Edit Card
36	- **Description**: Update a card's title, assignee, or description
37	- **Inputs**: Card ID, updated fields (partial)
38	- **Outputs**: Updated card persisted and broadcast
39	- **Behavior**: PATCH update, only modify provided fields, broadcast `card:updated` event
40	
41	#### Feature: Delete Card
42	- **Description**: Permanently remove a card and all its comments
43	- **Inputs**: Card ID
44	- **Outputs**: Card and comments removed from DB, broadcast to all clients
45	- **Behavior**: Delete card (cascade-deletes comments), broadcast `card:deleted` event
46	
47	#### Feature: View Board
48	- **Description**: Fetch all cards grouped by column with nested comments
49	- **Inputs**: None
50	- **Outputs**: All cards ordered by column and position, with comments nested
51	- **Behavior**: Single GET returning full board state on initial load
52	
53	---
54	
55	### Capability: Card Organization
56	Cards can be moved between columns and reordered within a column via drag and drop.
57	
58	#### Feature: Move Card Between Columns
59	- **Description**: Change a card's column (e.g. Ready → In Progress)
60	- **Inputs**: Card ID, target column, new position index
61	- **Outputs**: Updated card persisted and broadcast
62	- **Behavior**: Update column and position fields, recompute sibling positions if needed, broadcast `card:moved`
63	
64	#### Feature: Reorder Card Within Column
65	- **Description**: Change a card's vertical position within its current column
66	- **Inputs**: Card ID, new position index
67	- **Outputs**: Updated positions persisted and broadcast
68	- **Behavior**: Recompute positions of affected cards using fractional indexing; renormalize when gaps become too small
69	
70	---
71	
72	### Capability: Rich Text Editing
73	Card descriptions use a block-based Notion-like editor.
74	
75	#### Feature: Block Editor
76	- **Description**: Edit card descriptions using BlockNote, a Notion-style block editor
77	- **Inputs**: Existing description JSON (or empty), user edits
78	- **Outputs**: Updated description as BlockNote JSON
79	- **Behavior**: Supports headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks; content saved as JSON string in DB; rendered as read-only blocks in card tile view
80	
81	---
82	
83	### Capability: Commenting
84	Anyone can add comments to a card using a free-text author name.
85	
86	#### Feature: Add Comment
87	- **Description**: Post a comment on a card with an author name and text body
88	- **Inputs**: Card ID, author name (free text), comment text
89	- **Outputs**: Persisted comment with timestamp, broadcast to all clients
90	- **Behavior**: Insert comment into DB, return with created_at, broadcast `comment:created`
91	
92	#### Feature: View Comments
93	- **Description**: Display all comments on a card in chronological order
94	- **Inputs**: Card ID (included in board fetch or card detail fetch)
95	- **Outputs**: Ordered list of comments
96	- **Behavior**: Fetched as part of the board state payload, nested under each card
97	
98	---
99	
100	### Capability: Realtime Sync
101	All mutations are broadcast to every connected client via WebSocket.
102	
103	#### Feature: WebSocket Event Broadcasting
104	- **Description**: Push typed events to all connected clients after every DB write
105	- **Inputs**: Event type + payload from any API mutation
106	- **Outputs**: JSON event delivered to all active WebSocket connections
107	- **Behavior**: After each DB write, server calls `broadcast(event, payload)`; clients handle: `card:created`, `card:updated`, `card:deleted`, `card:moved`, `comment:created`; clients apply events to local state without a full reload
108	
109	---
110	
111	### Capability: Persistence
112	All board data is stored in a SQLite database on the server.
113	
114	#### Feature: Database Schema and Migrations
115	- **Description**: Define and initialize the SQLite schema on server startup
116	- **Inputs**: Migration SQL files
117	- **Outputs**: DB file with correct schema, ready for queries
118	- **Behavior**: Migration runner checks current schema version on boot and applies pending migrations
119	
120	---
121	
122	## Repository Structure
123	
124	```
125	kanban/
126	├── server/
127	│   ├── db/
128	│   │   ├── migrations/        # Numbered SQL migration files (001_init.sql, ...)
129	│   │   ├── schema.js          # Migration runner — reads DB version, applies pending migrations
130	│   │   └── queries.js         # All SQL query functions (no SQL outside this file)
131	│   ├── api/
132	│   │   ├── cards.js           # Express router: card CRUD routes
133	│   │   └── comments.js        # Express router: comment routes
134	│   ├── ws/
135	│   │   └── broadcaster.js     # WebSocket server init + broadcast(event, payload)
136	│   └── index.js               # Entry point: Express app, mounts routers, inits DB + WS
137	├── client/
138	│   ├── src/
139	│   │   ├── components/
140	│   │   │   ├── Board/
141	│   │   │   │   ├── Board.jsx         # Root board — dnd-kit DndContext, renders columns
142	│   │   │   │   ├── Column.jsx        # Single column (SortableContext droppable)
143	│   │   │   │   └── CardTile.jsx      # Card summary tile (useSortable draggable)
144	│   │   │   ├── CardModal/
145	│   │   │   │   ├── CardModal.jsx     # Card detail overlay — title, assignee, editor, comments
146	│   │   │   │   ├── BlockEditor.jsx   # BlockNote editor wrapper (edit + read-only mode)
147	│   │   │   │   └── CommentList.jsx   # Comment list + add-comment form
148	│   │   │   └── CreateCardForm.jsx    # Inline new-card form at bottom of "Ready" column
149	│   │   ├── hooks/
150	│   │   │   ├── useBoard.js           # Board state, mutation helpers (create/update/delete/move)
151	│   │   │   └── useWebSocket.js       # WS connection lifecycle + event dispatch to useBoard
152	│   │   ├── api/
153	│   │   │   └── client.js             # Typed fetch wrappers for all REST endpoints
154	│   │   └── App.jsx
155	│   ├── index.html
156	│   └── vite.config.js                # Dev proxy /api → :3001
157	├── Dockerfile                        # Multi-stage: build client, serve with Node
158	├── docker-compose.yml                # App + named volume for SQLite DB
159	└── package.json                      # Root workspace scripts: dev, build, start
160	```
161	
162	## Module Definitions
163	
164	### Module: `server/db`
165	- **Maps to capability**: Persistence
166	- **Responsibility**: SQLite initialization, migrations, and all query functions — no SQL exists outside this module
167	- **Exports**:
168	  - `initDb()` — opens DB file, runs pending migrations
169	  - `getCards()` — all cards with nested comments, ordered by column + position
170	  - `createCard(data)` — inserts card, returns it
171	  - `updateCard(id, data)` — partial update, returns updated card
172	  - `deleteCard(id)` — deletes card and cascade-deletes comments
173	  - `moveCard(id, column, position)` — updates column + position, renormalizes siblings if needed
174	  - `createComment(cardId, data)` — inserts comment, returns it
175	
176	### Module: `server/api`
177	- **Maps to capability**: Card Management, Commenting
178	- **Responsibility**: Express route handlers translating HTTP requests to DB queries and broadcasting events
179	- **Exports**: `cardsRouter`, `commentsRouter` (Express Routers)
180	
181	### Module: `server/ws`
182	- **Maps to capability**: Realtime Sync
183	- **Responsibility**: WebSocket server lifecycle and broadcasting typed events to all connected clients
184	- **Exports**:
185	  - `initWs(httpServer)` — attaches `ws` server
186	  - `broadcast(event, payload)` — sends `{ event, payload }` JSON to all active connections
187	
188	### Module: `client/src/hooks`
189	- **Maps to capability**: Card Management, Realtime Sync
190	- **Responsibility**: Board state management, REST mutations, WebSocket event application
191	- **Exports**:
192	  - `useBoard()` — returns `{ columns, createCard, updateCard, deleteCard, moveCard, addComment }`
193	  - `useWebSocket(onEvent)` — manages WS connection, calls `onEvent` for each incoming message
194	
195	### Module: `client/src/components/Board`
196	- **Maps to capability**: Card Organization
197	- **Responsibility**: Drag-and-drop board layout using dnd-kit; handles cross-column and within-column reordering
198	- **Exports**: `Board` component
199	
200	### Module: `client/src/components/CardModal`
201	- **Maps to capability**: Rich Text Editing, Commenting
202	- **Responsibility**: Card detail overlay with BlockNote editor and comments thread
203	- **Exports**: `CardModal` component
204	
205	---
206	
207	## Dependency Chain
208	
209	### Phase 0: Project Foundation
210	No dependencies — built first.
211	
212	- **Project scaffold**: Monorepo with `client/` and `server/` workspaces; root `package.json` with `dev` (parallel Vite + nodemon), `build`, and `start` scripts
213	- **Docker setup**: Multi-stage Dockerfile; `docker-compose.yml` with named SQLite volume; `NODE_ENV` switching
214	- **DB schema + migrations**: `server/db/migrations/001_init.sql` defining `cards` and `comments` tables; `schema.js` migration runner that runs on server startup
215	
216	### Phase 1: Data Layer
217	Depends on: Phase 0 (DB schema)
218	
219	- **`server/db/queries.js`**: All DB query functions — depends on DB schema
220	- **`server/api/cards.js` + `comments.js`**: REST routes (`GET /api/cards`, `POST /api/cards`, `PATCH /api/cards/:id`, `DELETE /api/cards/:id`, `POST /api/cards/:id/comments`) — depends on queries.js
221	
222	### Phase 2: Basic UI
223	Depends on: Phase 1 (REST API)
224	
225	- **`client/src/api/client.js`**: Typed fetch wrappers — depends on REST API contract
226	- **`useBoard` hook**: Board state loaded via REST, mutation helpers — depends on client.js
227	- **`Board`, `Column`, `CardTile`**: Static board rendering (no drag yet) — depends on useBoard
228	- **`CardModal`**: Opens on card click; shows title, assignee, description (read-only), comments — depends on useBoard
229	- **`CreateCardForm`**: Inline form at bottom of "Ready" column — depends on useBoard
230	
231	### Phase 3: Interactivity
232	Depends on: Phase 2 (basic UI)
233	
234	- **Drag-and-drop** (dnd-kit): Wire Board/Column/CardTile with dnd-kit; call `moveCard` on drop — depends on Board components, client.js
235	- **Block editor** (BlockNote): Integrate into CardModal description field; save on blur/close — depends on CardModal
236	- **Inline card editing**: Click-to-edit title and assignee on CardTile and in CardModal — depends on CardModal, CardTile
237	
238	### Phase 4: Realtime
239	Depends on: Phase 1 (REST API) + Phase 2 (basic UI)
240	
241	- **`server/ws/broadcaster.js`**: WebSocket server; call `broadcast` from each API mutation route — depends on server/api
242	- **`useWebSocket` hook**: Connect to WS, receive events, update useBoard state — depends on useBoard
243	
244	---
245	
246	## Development Phases
247	
248	### Phase 0: Project Foundation
249	**Goal**: Runnable skeleton — server responds, client loads, Docker works
250	
251	**Entry Criteria**: Empty repository
252	
253	**Tasks**:
254	- [ ] Initialize monorepo: root `package.json` with workspaces and `dev`/`build`/`start` scripts
255	  - Acceptance: `npm run dev` starts server on :3001 and client on :5173
256	- [ ] Setup Vite + React in `client/`
257	  - Acceptance: `http://localhost:5173` renders a React root without errors
258	- [ ] Setup Express in `server/index.js`
259	  - Acceptance: `GET /health` returns `{ ok: true }`
260	- [ ] DB schema: `migrations/001_init.sql` + `schema.js` migration runner
261	  - Acceptance: On server start, `kanban.db` is created with `cards` and `comments` tables
262	- [ ] Docker: multi-stage Dockerfile + `docker-compose.yml` with SQLite volume
263	  - Acceptance: `docker compose up` serves the app on :3000 with persistent data
264	
265	**Exit Criteria**: App runs in both dev mode and Docker; empty board is reachable
266	
267	---
268	
269	### Phase 1: Data Layer
270	**Goal**: Full CRUD API backed by persistent SQLite
271	
272	**Entry Criteria**: Phase 0 complete
273	
274	**Tasks**:
275	- [ ] `server/db/queries.js`: implement `getCards`, `createCard`, `updateCard`, `deleteCard`, `moveCard`, `createComment`
276	  - Acceptance: Unit tests for each function pass against an in-memory SQLite DB
277	- [ ] `server/api/cards.js`: `GET /api/cards`, `POST /api/cards`, `PATCH /api/cards/:id`, `DELETE /api/cards/:id`
278	  - Acceptance: Integration tests cover happy path + 404 for unknown ID + 400 for missing title
279	- [ ] `server/api/comments.js`: `POST /api/cards/:id/comments`
280	  - Acceptance: Comment is persisted and returned with created_at
281	
282	**Exit Criteria**: All CRUD operations work via REST; data survives server restart
283	
284	---
285	
286	### Phase 2: Basic UI
287	**Goal**: Board is visible and fully usable (no drag, no realtime yet)
288	
289	**Entry Criteria**: Phase 1 complete
290	
291	**Tasks**:
292	- [ ] `client/src/api/client.js`: typed fetch wrappers for all endpoints
293	- [ ] `useBoard` hook: load cards on mount, expose `createCard`, `updateCard`, `deleteCard`, `moveCard`, `addComment`
294	- [ ] `Board`, `Column`, `CardTile`: render three columns with cards in correct order
295	  - Acceptance: Cards fetched from API appear in the right column
296	- [ ] `CardModal`: open on card click; title, assignee (editable), description (read-only), comment list + form
297	- [ ] `CreateCardForm`: inline at bottom of "Ready" column; submits title + optional assignee
298	- [ ] Delete card: button in CardModal; card disappears from board
299	
300	**Exit Criteria**: A team member can create, view, edit, and delete cards in a browser
301	
302	---
303	
304	### Phase 3: Interactivity
305	**Goal**: Drag-and-drop, rich block editor, inline editing
306	
307	**Entry Criteria**: Phase 2 complete
308	
309	**Tasks**:
310	- [ ] Integrate dnd-kit: `DndContext` in Board, `SortableContext` in Column, `useSortable` in CardTile; handle cross-column and within-column drops
311	  - Acceptance: Drop updates card position and/or column via API; UI reflects new order without full reload; drag ghost visible during drag
312	- [ ] Integrate BlockNote into CardModal: replaces plain textarea; saves description JSON on modal close
313	  - Acceptance: User can write headings, bullets, code blocks; description renders correctly in view mode; JSON stored in DB
314	- [ ] Inline editing: click title or assignee on CardTile to edit in place
315	
316	**Exit Criteria**: Full interactive board — drag, rich text, comments all functional
317	
318	---
319	
320	### Phase 4: Realtime
321	**Goal**: All connected clients see changes live
322	
323	**Entry Criteria**: Phase 1 + Phase 3 complete
324	
325	**Tasks**:
326	- [ ] `server/ws/broadcaster.js`: attach `ws` to HTTP server, expose `broadcast(event, payload)`
327	- [ ] Call `broadcast` from each API route after every successful DB write (cards + comments)
328	- [ ] `useWebSocket` hook: open WS connection, dispatch incoming events to update `useBoard` state
329	  - Acceptance: Open two browser tabs — action in tab A appears in tab B within 500ms without refresh
330	
331	**Exit Criteria**: Realtime sync works for all event types across multiple clients
332	
333	---
334	
335	## Test Strategy
336	
337	### Test Pyramid
338	
339	```
340	        /\
341	       /E2E\        ← 10% (Playwright: create card, drag, comment, realtime)
342	      /------\
343	     /Integration\  ← 30% (supertest: API routes + DB round-trips)
344	    /------------\
345	   /  Unit Tests  \ ← 60% (DB queries, useBoard hook logic)
346	  /----------------\
347	```
348	
349	### Coverage Requirements
350	- Line coverage: 70% minimum
351	- Function coverage: 80% minimum
352	
353	### Critical Test Scenarios
354	
355	**`server/db/queries.js`**
356	- Happy path: create → get → update → delete card
357	- Edge case: `deleteCard` cascades to comments
358	- Edge case: `moveCard` correctly reorders sibling positions
359	- Edge case: position renormalization triggers when gap < 0.001
360	
361	**REST API (supertest)**
362	- Full CRUD round-trip for cards and comments
363	- 404 on unknown card ID
364	- 400 on missing required `title` field
365	
366	**`useBoard` hook**
367	- Board loads correctly from GET /api/cards on mount
368	- Incoming `card:moved` WebSocket event updates column state
369	
370	**E2E (Playwright)**
371	- Create a card in "Ready", drag it to "In Progress", verify column
372	- Open card, add comment, verify it appears in the comment list
373	- Open two tabs: create card in tab 1, verify it appears in tab 2 (realtime)
374	
375	---
376	
377	## System Components
378	
379	**Server (Node.js + Express)**
380	- HTTP server serving REST API + static client build in production
381	- WebSocket server on the same port (HTTP upgrade) for broadcasting
382	- SQLite via `better-sqlite3` (synchronous; no async complexity for single-writer workloads)
383	
384	**Client (React + Vite)**
385	- Single-page application; loads full board state on mount
386	- dnd-kit for drag-and-drop
387	- BlockNote for block-based rich editing
388	- Native WebSocket API for realtime sync
389	
390	**Database Schema**
391	
392	```sql
393	CREATE TABLE cards (
394	  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,           -- UUID v4
395	  title       TEXT    NOT NULL,
396	  assignee    TEXT,                          -- free text, nullable
397	  column      TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ready',  -- 'ready' | 'in_progress' | 'done'
398	  position    REAL    NOT NULL,              -- fractional index for ordering within column
399	  description TEXT,                         -- BlockNote JSON string, nullable
400	  created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL              -- Unix timestamp ms
401	);
402	
403	CREATE TABLE comments (
404	  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY,
405	  card_id     TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES cards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
406	  author      TEXT    NOT NULL,             -- free text display name
407	  content     TEXT    NOT NULL,
408	  created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
409	);
410	```
411	
412	## Technology Stack
413	
414	**Runtime**: Node.js 20 LTS
415	
416	**Backend**:
417	- `express` 4 — HTTP server and REST routing
418	- `ws` — WebSocket server (lightweight, no socket.io overhead)
419	- `better-sqlite3` — synchronous SQLite driver; ideal for single-writer server
420	- `uuid` — card and comment ID generation
421	
422	**Frontend**:
423	- React 18 + Vite 5
424	- `@dnd-kit/core` + `@dnd-kit/sortable` — drag and drop
425	- `@blocknote/react` — Notion-like block editor
426	- No global state library (useBoard + prop drilling is sufficient at this scale)
427	
428	**Deployment**:
429	- Docker multi-stage build (Node 20 Alpine: build stage for client, runtime stage for server)
430	- `docker-compose.yml` with a named volume mounting `data/kanban.db`
431	- Dev mode: `nodemon` for server HMR + Vite HMR for client; Vite proxies `/api` and `/ws` to :3001
432	
433	---
434	
435	## Risks and Mitigations
436	
437	**Risk**: BlockNote JSON format is opaque — difficult to query or migrate later
438	- **Impact**: Low — description search is not a current requirement
439	- **Likelihood**: Low — no near-term need to query description content
440	- **Mitigation**: Store as raw TEXT in SQLite; a migration can extract plain text if search is added later
441	
442	**Risk**: Fractional position ordering loses fidelity after many reorders
443	- **Impact**: Medium — cards could lose stable ordering after many operations
444	- **Likelihood**: Low for typical team board usage (< 100 cards)
445	- **Mitigation**: Renormalize positions (reset to 1, 2, 3...) when the gap between adjacent positions drops below 0.001
446	
447	**Risk**: No authentication means anyone with the URL can delete all cards
448	- **Impact**: High if URL leaks outside the intended team
449	- **Likelihood**: Low in a controlled internal server environment
450	- **Mitigation**: Document that the app must not be exposed to the public internet; a future `BOARD_PASSWORD` env var can enable HTTP Basic Auth with minimal code change
451	
452	**Risk**: dnd-kit cross-column drag complexity (hit detection, ghost positioning)
453	- **Impact**: Medium — poor UX if drag targets are ambiguous
454	- **Likelihood**: Medium — cross-container DnD requires careful setup
455	- **Mitigation**: Use dnd-kit's `closestCenter` collision detection + `rectIntersection` fallback; test on Chrome and Firefox before shipping
456	
457	---
458	
459	## Appendix
460	
461	### Open Questions
462	- Should the "Done" column auto-archive cards after N days? (Not in scope for MVP)
463	- Should comments support rich text, or is plain text sufficient? (Plain text for MVP)
464	- Should card creation be possible from any column, or only "Ready"? (MVP: "Ready" only; other columns receive cards via drag)
465	- Should there be a way to search or filter cards by assignee? (Not in scope for MVP)
466	
467	### Glossary
468	- **Column**: One of the three fixed board states — `ready`, `in_progress`, `done`
469	- **Card**: A task item with title, assignee, description, and comments
470	- **Position**: A floating-point value used to order cards within a column
471	- **Broadcast**: Sending a WebSocket event to all currently connected clients
472	- **BlockNote JSON**: The structured JSON format used by BlockNote to represent block-based document content
473

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me provide a complete summary:

---

## Project Exploration Complete: Kanban Board Application

### Project Overview
This is a **Kanban Board application** - a dead-simple, self-hosted task management tool designed for small teams (2-10 people) who want real-time collaboration without account overhead. The project is defined in `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/` as a monorepo with separate client and server workspaces.

---

## Root Directory Structure

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/`

**Root level files and directories:**
- `.env.example` - Environment configuration template (contains API keys for various providers)
- `.git` - Git metadata file
- `.gitignore` - Standard git ignore rules
- `.cursor/` - Cursor IDE config directory
- `.factory/` - Factory configuration and project documentation
  - `plans/` - Task planning documents
  - `prds/` - Product requirements documents (kanban-board.md is the main PRD)
  - `qa/` - QA reports
- `.taskmaster/` - Task master configuration and state
  - `config.json` - Model and global configuration
  - `tasks/` - Task definitions and state
  - `state.json` - Current task state
  - `reports/` - Task complexity reports
- `MEMORY.md` - Empty memory/notes file for the project
- `kanban/` - **Main application monorepo** (the actual project code)

---

## Kanban Monorepo Structure

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/kanban/`

### Root package.json
Located at: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/kanban/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "kanban-app",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["client", "server"],
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "concurrently -n server,client -c blue,green \"npm -w server run dev\" \"npm -w client run dev\"",
    "build": "npm -w client run build",
    "start": "npm -w server run start",
    "test:setup": "node --test test/setup.test.mjs"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "concurrently": "^8.2.2"
  }
}
```

**Key features:**
- npm workspaces with `client/` and `server/`
- `dev` script runs both server (port :3001) and client (port :5173) concurrently using `concurrently`
- `build` script builds client for production
- `start` script serves production build
- `test:setup` runs structural validation tests

### Directory Layout

```
kanban/
├── package.json
├── package-lock.json
├── client/                 ← React + Vite frontend
│   ├── package.json
│   └── src/
│       └── .gitkeep
├── server/                 ← Express + Node.js backend
│   ├── package.json
│   ├── db/                 ← Database layer (SQLite)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   ├── api/                ← REST API routes
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── ws/                 ← WebSocket server
│       └── .gitkeep
└── test/
    └── setup.test.mjs      ← Structural validation tests
```

---

## Client Workspace

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/kanban/client/`

### package.json
```json
{
  "name": "kanban-client",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview"
  },
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {}
}
```

**Current state:**
- Only `package.json` exists
- `src/` directory created but empty (only `.gitkeep`)
- No vite.config.js yet
- No dependencies installed yet (empty objects)

**Missing files to be created:**
- `vite.config.js` - Vite configuration with proxy settings for `/api` and `/ws` to :3001
- `index.html` - Entry point
- `src/App.jsx` - Root React component
- `src/components/` - Component hierarchy (Board, Column, CardTile, CardModal, CreateCardForm, BlockEditor, CommentList)
- `src/hooks/` - Custom hooks (useBoard, useWebSocket)
- `src/api/` - Fetch client wrapper

---

## Server Workspace

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/kanban/server/`

### package.json
```json
{
  "name": "kanban-server",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "nodemon index.js",
    "start": "node index.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {},
  "devDependencies": {}
}
```

**Current state:**
- Only `package.json` exists
- Subdirectories created:
  - `db/` - empty (`.gitkeep` only)
  - `api/` - empty (`.gitkeep` only)
  - `ws/` - empty (`.gitkeep` only)
- No dependencies installed yet (empty objects)

**Missing files to be created:**
- `index.js` - Entry point (Express app initialization)
- `db/migrations/` - SQL migration files (001_init.sql)
- `db/schema.js` - Migration runner
- `db/queries.js` - All SQL query functions
- `api/cards.js` - Card CRUD routes
- `api/comments.js` - Comment routes
- `ws/broadcaster.js` - WebSocket server and broadcasting

---

## Test Files

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/kanban/test/setup.test.mjs`

Contains structural validation tests using Node.js 20 built-in `node:test` module. Tests validate:
- Root `package.json` structure (private, name, version, workspaces)
- npm scripts (dev, build, start, test:setup with concurrently)
- Directory structure (client, server, db, api, ws, src)
- Workspace `package.json` files (client and server)

**All tests currently PASS** ✓ (Phase 0 is complete)

---

## Task Master / Project Management

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/.taskmaster/`

**Status of tasks:**
- Task 1: "Initialize monorepo structure and workspace configuration" - **DONE** (all 4 subtasks complete)
  - Create root package.json ✓
  - Set up npm scripts ✓
  - Create directory structure ✓
  - Initialize workspace package.json files ✓

- Task 2: "Setup Vite + React client application" - **PENDING** (5 subtasks, not started yet)
  - Initialize Vite React project
  - Configure vite.config.js
  - Set up basic App.jsx
  - Create index.html
  - Other client setup tasks

- Tasks 3-8: Remaining phases (Server setup, DB schema, API routes, Basic UI, Interactivity, Realtime) - PENDING

---

## Product Requirements Document (PRD)

**Path**: `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-2/.factory/prds/kanban-board.md`

**Key features:**
1. **Card Management** - Create, edit, delete, view cards with title, assignee, description
2. **Card Organization** - Drag-and-drop between columns and reorder within columns
3. **Rich Text Editing** - BlockNote editor for descriptions
4. **Commenting** - Add/view comments on cards with author names
5. **Realtime Sync** - WebSocket broadcasting of all mutations
6. **Persistence** - SQLite database with migrations

**Database schema:**
- `cards` table: id, title, assignee, column (ready/in_progress/done), position, description, created_at
- `comments` table: id, card_id, author, content, created_at

**Technology stack:**
- Backend: Node.js 20, Express 4, ws (WebSocket), better-sqlite3, uuid
- Frontend: React 18, Vite 5, dnd-kit, BlockNote
- Deployment: Docker + docker-compose with SQLite volume

**Development phases:**
- Phase 0: Project Foundation (COMPLETE)
- Phase 1: Data Layer (queued - not started)
- Phase 2: Basic UI (queued)
- Phase 3: Interactivity (queued)
- Phase 4: Realtime (queued)

---

## Git Status

**Branch**: `kanban-board/kanban-board-2`
**Status**: Working tree clean (all Phase 0 files committed)

---

## Summary

Now I'm looking at the structural tests in the test file and I see a critical issue: the test is trying to read the vite.config.js file synchronously at the describe block level before the file even exists, which will cause the entire test suite to fail before any individual tests can run. This is a logic flaw that needs to be fixed by wrapping the file read operation inside the actual test callback instead. I see the issue now—the structural test checks for `App.test.jsx` existence in step 2, but that file isn't created until step 8, so it will correctly fail in the RED phase. The real problem is that when the structural test runs and tries to read `vite.config.js` synchronously inside the `describe()` block, it'll crash the entire test suite if that file doesn't exist yet, rather than failing gracefully. I need to either move that file read into individual test cases or add an existence check so the tests can fail properly without crashing the runner. in step 7, we create `src/test-setup.js`. In step 8, we create `App.test.jsx`. But `App.jsx` isn't created until step 10. So running `npm -w client run test` in step 9 would fail because `App.test.jsx` imports `App.jsx` which doesn't exist. That's the intended RED state for the component tests.

But wait - step 14 says "Run GREEN: node --test test/client.setup.test.mjs → all structural tests pass". But the structural test checks for `client/src/App.test.jsx`. If we created `App.test.jsx` in step 8, then by step 14 it should exist. That's fine.

### 4. Missing: npm create vite vs manual setup

The task description says: "Run 'npm create vite@latest client -- --template react' in the client directory."

The plan instead manually creates all files (package.json, vite.config.js, index.html, etc.) without using the Vite scaffolding tool. The task also mentions cleaning up default template files and preparing the project structure for custom implementation. Using `npm create vite` would generate a full project with default assets, styles, and components, but the existing `kanban/client/package.json` already has a specific name ("kanban-client") and custom scripts, so running the scaffolding command would either overwrite this file or fail since the directory already exists.

The manual approach is actually more appropriate here because the `client/` directory already exists with configured files, the project has specific requirements, and using `npm create vite` in an existing directory would create conflicts. The plan should explicitly acknowledge this decision and explain why we're diverging from the task description.

There's also no mention of how to add a test command to the root `package.json` that runs the new `client.setup.test.mjs` file, though running it directly with `node --test` works fine for verification.

The `index.html` doesn't reference a favicon, which is clean and consistent with the plan.

There's a significant technical issue in the test file: the proxy settings describe block calls `readFileSync` at the describe-level rather than inside an `it()` block, which means the file read happens during test collection instead of during test execution.

Fixing this...

The key issue is that moving `readFileSync` into each test callback ensures the file read happens during the test execution phase rather than during suite setup, so if the file doesn't exist in the RED phase, it fails the individual test gracefully instead of crashing the entire suite.

There's also an inconsistency in the plan—the task specifies using `npm create vite@latest client -- --template react`, but the plan doesn't explain why this approach isn't being used or acknowledge that the client directory already exists with a configured package.json, which would make manual setup more appropriate.

HMR verification is inherently manual and can't be tested with unit tests, which the plan does acknowledge in its verification section, so that's acceptable. However, there's a gap around whether the root package.json's test script should be updated to run the new client setup test alongside the existing setup test.

Looking at the Vitest configuration with globals enabled...

the test file explicitly imports describe, it, and expect from vitest, which is redundant but not wrong—it's actually more explicit. Since this is a JavaScript project without TypeScript, there won't be any type-checking issues either way.

Then checking main.jsx, I notice it doesn't directly import App.css, but that's fine because App.jsx imports it internally, so the stylesheet will still load when the component is rendered.

The plan also skips creating a separate index.css for global styles and puts everything in App.css instead, which works for this minimal setup.

Looking at the actual issues that need fixing: the readFileSync call in the vite.config.js proxy settings happens at the describe level and will crash during the RED phase when the file doesn't exist yet—that needs to move inside the test callbacks. The root package.json is also missing a test:setup script that should run the client setup test file. And there are a few other gaps to address.

Let me review the plan more carefully to identify what actually needs updating versus what's just a minor inconsistency.

The critical issue is that `readFileSync` at the describe level will cause the entire test block to fail during the RED phase when `vite.config.js` doesn't exist yet—this breaks the TDD workflow. I also need to update the root test setup script to include the new test file. As for why `npm create vite` isn't being used despite the task description mentioning it, that's because the `client/` directory already exists with a configured `package.json`, so I should clarify that in the plan. I need to move the file reading inside the test callbacks to avoid issues with node:test's error handling, and I'm realizing there's a gap in the plan—the root `package.json` script needs to be updated to actually run the new client setup tests, otherwise they won't execute when developers run `npm run test:setup`. RTL appends elements to the document by default, so the `.app` selector should find the element correctly and `toBeInTheDocument()` will pass. CSS imports are also handled automatically by Vite/Vitest in the test environment, so that's not an issue either.

Now let me finalize the plan with those corrections about the test script and the readFileSync placement.

content.thinking:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the plan and its issues. Let me identify the problems:

1. **Critical Bug: `readFileSync` called at describe-level in the proxy tests (Subtask 2a)**

In the test code for Subtask 2a:
```js
describe('vite.config.js proxy settings', () => {
  const configText = readFileSync(resolve(ROOT, 'client/vite.config.js'), 'utf-8');
  ...
```

The `readFileSync` call is at the top level of the `describe()` callback. 

When `vite.config.js` doesn't exist (RED state), this throws `ENOENT` synchronously inside the `describe` callback, which crashes that describe block in an uncontrolled way rather than producing clean test failures. I need to move `readFileSync` inside each `it()` callback so the error is thrown within a test context and can fail cleanly.

2. **Missing: Root `package.json` `test:setup` script should be updated**

The root `package.json` has `"test:setup": "node --test test/setup.test.mjs"`, but the plan creates a new test file `test/client.setup.test.mjs` that also needs to be run as part of the setup phase.

**Fix**: Update root `package.json`'s `test:setup` script to `node --test test/*.test.mjs` so it picks up all test files.

3. **Inconsistency: `client.setup.test.mjs` is written as two separate snippets with different test sections (Subtasks 1a and 2a) but there's no instruction to merge them or confirm the final combined file content**

The plan tells the agent to create `kanban/test/client.setup.test.mjs` with dependency/files tests in Subtask 1a, then extend it with proxy tests in Subtask 2a. However, the execution order and final merged state of the file isn't explicitly confirmed, which could lead to confusion about what the complete test file should contain. I see the tension now—step 1 says "all node:test tests" which should include the proxy tests, but if I create them upfront, they'll fail immediately in RED because the file they're trying to read doesn't exist yet. The real solution is to move the `readFileSync` call inside the test itself so it only runs when the test executes, not when the file loads, which also resolves the ordering confusion between creating the initial test file and extending it in step 2a. I'm realizing there's a critical gap in the execution order—the plan never explicitly states when to add the proxy tests to the client.setup.test.mjs file, even though step 14 expects them to be there and passing. The readFileSync issue at the describe level also needs fixing since it'll crash rather than fail cleanly when vite.config.js doesn't exist yet. The substring checks all look correct—`ws: true` will match even with the trailing comma, and the path and URL checks will find their targets in the config. Now I'm thinking through what other gaps might exist in the plan.

The test setup file path resolves correctly since it's relative to the vite.config.js location in the client directory. For the app container test, the querySelector will find the `.app` div that App renders, and since that div is attached to the DOM through the container, toBeInTheDocument should pass.

In Vitest with jsdom, document.body does exist and RTL appends to it by default, so that's not an issue. The RED step in the TDD flow is actually valid—when App.jsx doesn't exist yet, the import fails with a module error, which counts as a test failure even though it's not an assertion failure.

I'm realizing I should consolidate the proxy tests into the initial file creation rather than adding a separate extension step, and I need to move the readFileSync calls inside the test callbacks to avoid timing issues. The key fixes are addressing the readFileSync problem, combining the proxy tests upfront, adding a step to update the root package.json's test:setup script, and reordering the execution flow accordingly.

content.text:
I found three genuine issues that would cause the plan to fail or be incomplete:

1. **Critical bug (Subtask 2a)**: The `readFileSync` is called at the `describe()` level — outside any `it()` callback. When `vite.config.js` doesn't exist yet (RED state), this throws `ENOENT` synchronously inside the `describe` block, crashing the whole test suite uncontrollably instead of producing clean per-test failures. Each file read must be inside its `it()`.

2. **Missing execution step**: The proxy tests from Subtask 2a are presented as a separate "extend" step, but the Execution Order section never includes this step — so an agent executing the plan linearly would skip adding them to the test file entirely.

3. **Root `test:setup` script never updated**: The plan creates `test/client.setup.test.mjs` but never updates root `package.json`'s `test:setup` script (currently hardcoded to `node --test test/setup.test.mjs`). Running `npm run test:setup` from the workspace root would continue ignoring the new test file.

content.text:
The plan looks correct and complete. Here's a summary of the three issues I found and fixed:

1. **Critical bug fixed (readFileSync crash in RED state)**: The original proxy-test block called `readFileSync` at the `describe()` level. When `vite.config.js` doesn't exist, this would throw `ENOENT` synchronously before any `it()` runs — crashing the test suite rather than producing clean per-test failures. Every file read is now inside its own `it()` callback, isolating failures to individual tests.

2. **Missing execution step added**: The proxy tests were written as a separate "extend" step (Subtask 2a) that never appeared in the Execution Order section. The plan now creates `client.setup.test.mjs` as one complete file upfront (all three test groups in one pass), and the Execution Order reflects this. The orphaned "extend" instruction is removed.

3. **Root `package.json` update added**: The `test:setup` script was hardcoded to `node --test test/setup.test.mjs`, so `client.setup.test.mjs` would have been silently ignored by `npm run test:setup`. Step 4 in the Execution Order now explicitly changes that to `node --test test/*.test.mjs`, and the file is listed in the Critical Files table.

```

The `existsSync` check returns false rather than throwing, so each test assertion will fail cleanly if a file is missing — that's working as intended.

**Issue 2: Execution order step 10 - can we actually run vitest RED before App.jsx exists?**

Writing the test loop...

The `@testing-library/jest-dom` version works with Vitest, and importing it in the test setup file is the correct approach.

**Issue 10: Test setup file ordering**

The test setup file is created before the test file that depends on it, which is the right sequence. But I'm noticing Step 10 mentions running the RED phase where App tests should fail...

Actually, that makes sense—the failure would be from the missing `App.jsx` file itself, not from the setup configuration. The test infrastructure is properly initialized.

**Issue 11 & 12: Structural test requirements**

**Issue 13: React 18 compatibility**

`@testing-library/react` v16 is fully compatible with React 18 since it uses `createRoot` internally, so there's no peer dependency conflict here.

**Issue 14: Redundant imports with `globals: true`**

The Vitest config enables `globals: true`, which makes test utilities available without imports, but `App.test.jsx` explicitly imports `describe`, `it`, and `expect` anyway. This is unnecessary but harmless — explicit imports work fine alongside the global setting.

**Issue 15: Plugin version compatibility**

`@vitejs/plugin-react ^4.3.1` requires Vite 4.x or 5.x, and since the plan specifies `vite ^5.3.4`, they're compatible.

**Issue 16: jsdom compatibility**

Checking jsdom version compatibility... test 2.x?**

Vitest 2.x with `environment: 'jsdom'` internally uses jsdom. However, when specifying jsdom as a devDependency, this is used by Vitest. Vitest 2.x supports jsdom ^24. ✓

**Issue 17: The `for` loop creates `it()` tests using closure variable `f`**

Using `const f` in the `for...of` loop creates a new binding for each iteration, so the closure captures the correct value of `f` for each test. This is fine. ✓

**Issue 18: `npm run test:setup` glob and Node.js test runner**

The plan changes `test:setup` to use `node --test` with a glob pattern. The shell expands `test/*.test.mjs` before Node.js sees it, so this becomes multiple file arguments. This works on Unix systems, but I'm wondering about Windows compatibility since npm scripts run through `sh -c` and Windows might not have `sh` available.

Actually, thinking through this more carefully — the glob expansion happens in the shell, so on Unix it works fine. If only one test file exists initially, the glob still expands correctly to just that file. This approach is safe and won't cause issues. ✓

Now for the Node.js version compatibility: the project targets Node.js 20, and while `node --test` doesn't natively support glob patterns until Node.js 21, the shell expansion via `sh -c` happens before Node.js receives the arguments. So `sh -c "node --test test/*.test.mjs"` expands to `node --test test/setup.test.mjs test/client.setup.test.mjs`, and Node.js 20 handles multiple file arguments just fine.

1	# Task 1: Initialize Monorepo Structure and Workspace Configuration
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	This is the foundational task for a Kanban board application. The workspace at
6	`/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-1` is completely empty
7	(no application code, no package.json files). We need to scaffold the full monorepo
8	structure under a `kanban/` subdirectory, following the PRD at
9	`.factory/prds/kanban-board.md`.
10	
11	**Tech stack** (from PRD):
12	- Node.js 20 LTS runtime
13	- Server: Express 4, ws, better-sqlite3, uuid (populated in later tasks)
14	- Client: React 18 + Vite 5, dnd-kit, BlockNote (populated in later tasks)
15	- Server tests: Jest + supertest
16	- Client tests: Vitest (natural Vite companion)
17	- E2E: Playwright
18	
19	**TDD Note**: Because this is a structural/configuration task, tests validate the
20	filesystem and JSON structure rather than application logic. We use Node.js 20's
21	built-in `node:test` module — no test framework install needed for the validation phase.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Final Directory Target
26	
27	```
28	kanban/
29	├── package.json          ← root workspace (private, workspaces: [client, server])
30	├── test/
31	│   └── setup.test.mjs    ← structural validation tests (written FIRST)
32	├── client/
33	│   ├── package.json      ← kanban-client workspace
34	│   └── src/
35	│       └── .gitkeep
36	└── server/
37	    ├── package.json      ← kanban-server workspace
38	    ├── db/
39	    │   └── .gitkeep
40	    ├── api/
41	    │   └── .gitkeep
42	    └── ws/
43	        └── .gitkeep
44	```
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Phase 0 — Bootstrap: Write All Tests First (Red Phase)
49	
50	Before creating any implementation files, create the test file. Running it at this
51	point must fail for every test case.
52	
53	### File: `kanban/test/setup.test.mjs`
54	
55	Uses only Node.js 20 built-ins (`node:test`, `node:assert`, `node:fs`, `node:path`).
56	
57	```javascript
58	import { describe, it } from 'node:test';
59	import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
60	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
61	import { resolve, dirname } from 'node:path';
62	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
63	
64	const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
65	const ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..');  // kanban/ root
66	
67	function readPkg(rel) {
68	  return JSON.parse(readFileSync(resolve(ROOT, rel), 'utf-8'));
69	}
70	
71	// ── Subtask 1: Root package.json ─────────────────────────────────────────────
72	describe('Root package.json', () => {
73	  it('file exists', () => assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, 'package.json'))));
74	  it('has private: true',       () => assert.equal(readPkg('package.json').private, true));
75	  it('has name "kanban-app"',   () => assert.equal(readPkg('package.json').name, 'kanban-app'));
76	  it('has version "1.0.0"',     () => assert.equal(readPkg('package.json').version, '1.0.0'));
77	  it('workspaces contains "client"', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').workspaces?.includes('client')));
78	  it('workspaces contains "server"', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').workspaces?.includes('server')));
79	});
80	
81	// ── Subtask 2: npm scripts & concurrently ────────────────────────────────────
82	describe('Root npm scripts', () => {
83	  it('has scripts.dev', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').scripts?.dev));
84	  it('scripts.dev uses concurrently', () => assert.match(readPkg('package.json').scripts.dev, /concurrently/));
85	  it('scripts.dev references client dev', () => assert.match(readPkg('package.json').scripts.dev, /client/));
86	  it('scripts.dev references server dev', () => assert.match(readPkg('package.json').scripts.dev, /server/));
87	  it('has scripts.build', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').scripts?.build));
88	  it('has scripts.start', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').scripts?.start));
89	  it('devDependencies includes concurrently', () =>
90	    assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').devDependencies?.concurrently));
91	});
92	
93	// ── Subtask 3: Directory structure ───────────────────────────────────────────
94	describe('Directory structure', () => {
95	  const dirs = [
96	    'client',
97	    'client/src',
98	    'server',
99	    'server/db',
100	    'server/api',
101	    'server/ws',
102	  ];
103	  for (const dir of dirs) {
104	    it(`directory exists: ${dir}`, () =>
105	      assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, dir)), `Missing: kanban/${dir}/`));
106	  }
107	});
108	
109	// ── Subtask 4: Workspace package.json files ──────────────────────────────────
110	describe('Client package.json', () => {
111	  it('file exists', () => assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, 'client/package.json'))));
112	  it('name is "kanban-client"', () => assert.equal(readPkg('client/package.json').name, 'kanban-client'));
113	  it('has scripts.dev',     () => assert.ok(readPkg('client/package.json').scripts?.dev));
114	  it('has scripts.build',   () => assert.ok(readPkg('client/package.json').scripts?.build));
115	  it('has scripts.preview', () => assert.ok(readPkg('client/package.json').scripts?.preview));
116	});
117	
118	describe('Server package.json', () => {
119	  it('file exists', () => assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, 'server/package.json'))));
120	  it('name is "kanban-server"', () => assert.equal(readPkg('server/package.json').name, 'kanban-server'));
121	  it('has scripts.dev',   () => assert.ok(readPkg('server/package.json').scripts?.dev));
122	  it('has scripts.start', () => assert.ok(readPkg('server/package.json').scripts?.start));
123	});
124	```
125	
126	**How to run the tests** (works without installing anything because Node 20 ships `node:test`):
127	```bash
128	cd kanban-board-1
129	node --test kanban/test/setup.test.mjs
130	```
131	
132	Expected output at this point: **all tests fail** (files/dirs don't exist).
133	
134	---
135	
136	## Subtask 1 — Root package.json (Green Phase)
137	
138	### File to create: `kanban/package.json`
139	
140	```json
141	{
142	  "name": "kanban-app",
143	  "version": "1.0.0",
144	  "private": true,
145	  "workspaces": ["client", "server"],
146	  "scripts": {
147	    "dev": "concurrently -n server,client -c blue,green \"npm -w server run dev\" \"npm -w client run dev\"",
148	    "build": "npm -w client run build",
149	    "start": "npm -w server run start",
150	    "test:setup": "node --test test/setup.test.mjs"
151	  },
152	  "devDependencies": {
153	    "concurrently": "^8.2.2"
154	  }
155	}
156	```
157	
158	**Key decisions:**
159	- `private: true` prevents accidental publish of the root package
160	- `workspaces: ["client", "server"]` (relative to kanban/) enables `npm -w` targeting
161	- `concurrently` version ^8.2.2 (stable, widely used, supports `-n`/`-c` flags)
162	- Scripts use `npm -w <workspace> run <script>` pattern (standard npm workspaces)
163	- `test:setup` script makes the structural test runnable via npm
164	
165	**After writing this file**, run tests again:
166	- ✅ Subtask 1 tests pass
167	- ✅ Subtask 2 tests pass (all checks are JSON reads; they verify field presence, not runtime install)
168	- ❌ Subtask 3 tests still fail (no directories)
169	- ❌ Subtask 4 tests still fail (no workspace package.json files)
170	
171	---
172	
173	## Subtask 2 — Install concurrently (deferred to end)
174	
175	The JSON fields (`scripts.dev`, `devDependencies.concurrently`) are written as part of
176	Subtask 1 (`kanban/package.json`), so all Subtask 2 tests are green as soon as that
177	file is created.
178	
179	**`npm install` must NOT be run until Subtasks 3 and 4 are complete.** npm workspaces
180	resolves all workspace packages during install — if `kanban/client/package.json` or
181	`kanban/server/package.json` don't exist yet, npm fails with:
182	```
183	npm error Missing local dependency: client
184	```
185	
186	The actual install command is run as the very last step after all files are in place.
187	See **Final Verification** below.
188	
189	---
190	
191	## Subtask 3 — Directory Structure (Green Phase)
192	
193	Create all required directories. Use `.gitkeep` files to make empty directories
194	trackable by git.
195	
196	### Files to create:
197	- `kanban/client/src/.gitkeep` (empty file)
198	- `kanban/server/db/.gitkeep` (empty file)
199	- `kanban/server/api/.gitkeep` (empty file)
200	- `kanban/server/ws/.gitkeep` (empty file)
201	
202	Creating these files implicitly creates their parent directories.
203	
204	**After creating .gitkeep files**, run tests:
205	- ✅ All Subtask 3 directory tests pass
206	
207	---
208	
209	## Subtask 4 — Workspace package.json Files (Green Phase)
210	
211	### File: `kanban/client/package.json`
212	
213	```json
214	{
215	  "name": "kanban-client",
216	  "version": "1.0.0",
217	  "type": "module",
218	  "scripts": {
219	    "dev": "vite",
220	    "build": "vite build",
221	    "preview": "vite preview"
222	  },
223	  "dependencies": {},
224	  "devDependencies": {}
225	}
226	```
227	
228	**Key decisions:**
229	- `type: "module"` — Vite 5 works best with ESM
230	- Scripts match standard Vite project conventions
231	- Dependencies left empty (populated in later tasks when React/Vite are installed)
232	
233	### File: `kanban/server/package.json`
234	
235	```json
236	{
237	  "name": "kanban-server",
238	  "version": "1.0.0",
239	  "main": "index.js",
240	  "scripts": {
241	    "dev": "nodemon index.js",
242	    "start": "node index.js"
243	  },
244	  "dependencies": {},
245	  "devDependencies": {}
246	}
247	```
248	
249	**Key decisions:**
250	- No `type: "module"` — Express 4 / CommonJS convention
251	- `dev` uses `nodemon` for hot reload during development (installed in later tasks)
252	- `start` uses plain `node` for production
253	- `main: "index.js"` matches the PRD entry point
254	- Dependencies left empty (populated in later tasks when Express/ws/etc. are installed)
255	
256	**After creating both files**, run tests:
257	- ✅ All Subtask 4 tests pass
258

3	## Problem Statement
4	
5	Team collaboration tools like Jira, Trello, and Linear are powerful but heavyweight: they require account creation, have complex UIs, and introduce friction for small teams who just want to track work. This project provides a dead-simple, self-hosted kanban board accessible via a shared URL — no login, no setup for team members, just open and use.
6	
7	## Target Users
8	
9	Small development or project teams (2–10 people) who:
10	- Work in a trusted environment (same company or project)
11	- Want a visual task board without account management overhead
12	- Need to see each other's changes in real time
13	- Are comfortable with markdown/rich text for task descriptions
14	
15	## Success Metrics
16	
17	- Any team member can create a card, assign it, and move it to "Done" in under 30 seconds
18	- All connected clients reflect any card change within 500ms
19	- Zero accounts required — share a URL and you're in
20	- The app starts with a single `docker compose up` command
21	
22	---
23	
24	## Capability Tree
25	
26	### Capability: Card Management
27	Full lifecycle management of task cards on the board.
28	
29	#### Feature: Create Card
30	- **Description**: Create a new card with a title, optional assignee name, and optional description
31	- **Inputs**: Title (required), assignee (optional free text), column (defaults to "ready")
32	- **Outputs**: Persisted card with unique ID returned to client
33	- **Behavior**: Insert card into DB, assign a position at end of target column, broadcast `card:created` event
34	
35	#### Feature: Edit Card
36	- **Description**: Update a card's title, assignee, or description
37	- **Inputs**: Card ID, updated fields (partial)
38	- **Outputs**: Updated card persisted and broadcast
39	- **Behavior**: PATCH update, only modify provided fields, broadcast `card:updated` event
40	
41	#### Feature: Delete Card
42	- **Description**: Permanently remove a card and all its comments
43	- **Inputs**: Card ID
44	- **Outputs**: Card and comments removed from DB, broadcast to all clients
45	- **Behavior**: Delete card (cascade-deletes comments), broadcast `card:deleted` event
46	
47	#### Feature: View Board
48	- **Description**: Fetch all cards grouped by column with nested comments
49	- **Inputs**: None
50	- **Outputs**: All cards ordered by column and position, with comments nested
51	- **Behavior**: Single GET returning full board state on initial load
52	
53	---
54	
55	### Capability: Card Organization
56	Cards can be moved between columns and reordered within a column via drag and drop.
57	
58	#### Feature: Move Card Between Columns
59	- **Description**: Change a card's column (e.g. Ready → In Progress)
60	- **Inputs**: Card ID, target column, new position index
61	- **Outputs**: Updated card persisted and broadcast
62	- **Behavior**: Update column and position fields, recompute sibling positions if needed, broadcast `card:moved`
63	
64	#### Feature: Reorder Card Within Column
65	- **Description**: Change a card's vertical position within its current column
66	- **Inputs**: Card ID, new position index
67	- **Outputs**: Updated positions persisted and broadcast
68	- **Behavior**: Recompute positions of affected cards using fractional indexing; renormalize when gaps become too small
69	
70	---
71	
72	### Capability: Rich Text Editing
73	Card descriptions use a block-based Notion-like editor.
74	
75	#### Feature: Block Editor
76	- **Description**: Edit card descriptions using BlockNote, a Notion-style block editor
77	- **Inputs**: Existing description JSON (or empty), user edits
78	- **Outputs**: Updated description as BlockNote JSON
79	- **Behavior**: Supports headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks; content saved as JSON string in DB; rendered as read-only blocks in card tile view
80	
81	---
82	
83	### Capability: Commenting
84	Anyone can add comments to a card using a free-text author name.
85	
86	#### Feature: Add Comment
87	- **Description**: Post a comment on a card with an author name and text body
88	- **Inputs**: Card ID, author name (free text), comment text
89	- **Outputs**: Persisted comment with timestamp, broadcast to all clients
90	- **Behavior**: Insert comment into DB, return with created_at, broadcast `comment:created`
91	
92	#### Feature: View Comments
93	- **Description**: Display all comments on a card in chronological order
94	- **Inputs**: Card ID (included in board fetch or card detail fetch)
95	- **Outputs**: Ordered list of comments
96	- **Behavior**: Fetched as part of the board state payload, nested under each card
97	
98	---
99	
100	### Capability: Realtime Sync
101	All mutations are broadcast to every connected client via WebSocket.
102	
103	#### Feature: WebSocket Event Broadcasting
104	- **Description**: Push typed events to all connected clients after every DB write
105	- **Inputs**: Event type + payload from any API mutation
106	- **Outputs**: JSON event delivered to all active WebSocket connections
107	- **Behavior**: After each DB write, server calls `broadcast(event, payload)`; clients handle: `card:created`, `card:updated`, `card:deleted`, `card:moved`, `comment:created`; clients apply events to local state without a full reload
108	
109	---
110	
111	### Capability: Persistence
112	All board data is stored in a SQLite database on the server.
113	
114	#### Feature: Database Schema and Migrations
115	- **Description**: Define and initialize the SQLite schema on server startup
116	- **Inputs**: Migration SQL files
117	- **Outputs**: DB file with correct schema, ready for queries
118	- **Behavior**: Migration runner checks current schema version on boot and applies pending migrations
119	
120	---
121	
122	## Repository Structure
123	
124	```
125	kanban/
126	├── server/
127	│   ├── db/
128	│   │   ├── migrations/        # Numbered SQL migration files (001_init.sql, ...)
129	│   │   ├── schema.js          # Migration runner — reads DB version, applies pending migrations
130	│   │   └── queries.js         # All SQL query functions (no SQL outside this file)
131	│   ├── api/
132	│   │   ├── cards.js           # Express router: card CRUD routes
133	│   │   └── comments.js        # Express router: comment routes
134	│   ├── ws/
135	│   │   └── broadcaster.js     # WebSocket server init + broadcast(event, payload)
136	│   └── index.js               # Entry point: Express app, mounts routers, inits DB + WS
137	├── client/
138	│   ├── src/
139	│   │   ├── components/
140	│   │   │   ├── Board/
141	│   │   │   │   ├── Board.jsx         # Root board — dnd-kit DndContext, renders columns
142	│   │   │   │   ├── Column.jsx        # Single column (SortableContext droppable)
143	│   │   │   │   └── CardTile.jsx      # Card summary tile (useSortable draggable)
144	│   │   │   ├── CardModal/
145	│   │   │   │   ├── CardModal.jsx     # Card detail overlay — title, assignee, editor, comments
146	│   │   │   │   ├── BlockEditor.jsx   # BlockNote editor wrapper (edit + read-only mode)
147	│   │   │   │   └── CommentList.jsx   # Comment list + add-comment form
148	│   │   │   └── CreateCardForm.jsx    # Inline new-card form at bottom of "Ready" column
149	│   │   ├── hooks/
150	│   │   │   ├── useBoard.js           # Board state, mutation helpers (create/update/delete/move)
151	│   │   │   └── useWebSocket.js       # WS connection lifecycle + event dispatch to useBoard
152	│   │   ├── api/
153	│   │   │   └── client.js             # Typed fetch wrappers for all REST endpoints
154	│   │   └── App.jsx
155	│   ├── index.html
156	│   └── vite.config.js                # Dev proxy /api → :3001
157	├── Dockerfile                        # Multi-stage: build client, serve with Node
158	├── docker-compose.yml                # App + named volume for SQLite DB
159	└── package.json                      # Root workspace scripts: dev, build, start
160	```
161	
162	## Module Definitions
163	
164	### Module: `server/db`
165	- **Maps to capability**: Persistence
166	- **Responsibility**: SQLite initialization, migrations, and all query functions — no SQL exists outside this module
167	- **Exports**:
168	  - `initDb()` — opens DB file, runs pending migrations
169	  - `getCards()` — all cards with nested comments, ordered by column + position
170	  - `createCard(data)` — inserts card, returns it
171	  - `updateCard(id, data)` — partial update, returns updated card
172	  - `deleteCard(id)` — deletes card and cascade-deletes comments
173	  - `moveCard(id, column, position)` — updates column + position, renormalizes siblings if needed
174	  - `createComment(cardId, data)` — inserts comment, returns it
175	
176	### Module: `server/api`
177	- **Maps to capability**: Card Management, Commenting
178	- **Responsibility**: Express route handlers translating HTTP requests to DB queries and broadcasting events
179	- **Exports**: `cardsRouter`, `commentsRouter` (Express Routers)
180	
181	### Module: `server/ws`
182	- **Maps to capability**: Realtime Sync
183	- **Responsibility**: WebSocket server lifecycle and broadcasting typed events to all connected clients
184	- **Exports**:
185	  - `initWs(httpServer)` — attaches `ws` server
186	  - `broadcast(event, payload)` — sends `{ event, payload }` JSON to all active connections
187	
188	### Module: `client/src/hooks`
189	- **Maps to capability**: Card Management, Realtime Sync
190	- **Responsibility**: Board state management, REST mutations, WebSocket event application
191	- **Exports**:
192	  - `useBoard()` — returns `{ columns, createCard, updateCard, deleteCard, moveCard, addComment }`
193	  - `useWebSocket(onEvent)` — manages WS connection, calls `onEvent` for each incoming message
194	
195	### Module: `client/src/components/Board`
196	- **Maps to capability**: Card Organization
197	- **Responsibility**: Drag-and-drop board layout using dnd-kit; handles cross-column and within-column reordering
198	- **Exports**: `Board` component
199	
200	### Module: `client/src/components/CardModal`

content.content:
1	# Task 1: Initialize Monorepo Structure and Workspace Configuration
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	This is the foundational task for a Kanban board application. The workspace at
6	`/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-1` is completely empty
7	(no application code, no package.json files). We need to scaffold the full monorepo
8	structure under a `kanban/` subdirectory, following the PRD at
9	`.factory/prds/kanban-board.md`.
10	
11	**Tech stack** (from PRD):
12	- Node.js 20 LTS runtime
13	- Server: Express 4, ws, better-sqlite3, uuid (populated in later tasks)
14	- Client: React 18 + Vite 5, dnd-kit, BlockNote (populated in later tasks)
15	- Server tests: Jest + supertest
16	- Client tests: Vitest (natural Vite companion)
17	- E2E: Playwright
18	
19	**TDD Note**: Because this is a structural/configuration task, tests validate the
20	filesystem and JSON structure rather than application logic. We use Node.js 20's
21	built-in `node:test` module — no test framework install needed for the validation phase.
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Final Directory Target
26	
27	```
28	kanban/
29	├── package.json          ← root workspace (private, workspaces: [client, server])
30	├── test/
31	│   └── setup.test.mjs    ← structural validation tests (written FIRST)
32	├── client/
33	│   ├── package.json      ← kanban-client workspace
34	│   └── src/
35	│       └── .gitkeep
36	└── server/
37	    ├── package.json      ← kanban-server workspace
38	    ├── db/
39	    │   └── .gitkeep
40	    ├── api/
41	    │   └── .gitkeep
42	    └── ws/
43	        └── .gitkeep
44	```
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Phase 0 — Bootstrap: Write All Tests First (Red Phase)
49	
50	Before creating any implementation files, create the test file. Running it at this
51	point must fail for every test case.
52	
53	### File: `kanban/test/setup.test.mjs`
54	
55	Uses only Node.js 20 built-ins (`node:test`, `node:assert`, `node:fs`, `node:path`).
56	
57	```javascript
58	import { describe, it } from 'node:test';
59	import assert from 'node:assert/strict';
60	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
61	import { resolve, dirname } from 'node:path';
62	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
63	
64	const __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
65	const ROOT = resolve(__dirname, '..');  // kanban/ root
66	
67	function readPkg(rel) {
68	  return JSON.parse(readFileSync(resolve(ROOT, rel), 'utf-8'));
69	}
70	
71	// ── Subtask 1: Root package.json ─────────────────────────────────────────────
72	describe('Root package.json', () => {
73	  it('file exists', () => assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, 'package.json'))));
74	  it('has private: true',       () => assert.equal(readPkg('package.json').private, true));
75	  it('has name "kanban-app"',   () => assert.equal(readPkg('package.json').name, 'kanban-app'));
76	  it('has version "1.0.0"',     () => assert.equal(readPkg('package.json').version, '1.0.0'));
77	  it('workspaces contains "client"', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').workspaces?.includes('client')));
78	  it('workspaces contains "server"', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').workspaces?.includes('server')));
79	});
80	
81	// ── Subtask 2: npm scripts & concurrently ────────────────────────────────────
82	describe('Root npm scripts', () => {
83	  it('has scripts.dev', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').scripts?.dev));
84	  it('scripts.dev uses concurrently', () => assert.match(readPkg('package.json').scripts.dev, /concurrently/));
85	  it('scripts.dev references client dev', () => assert.match(readPkg('package.json').scripts.dev, /client/));
86	  it('scripts.dev references server dev', () => assert.match(readPkg('package.json').scripts.dev, /server/));
87	  it('has scripts.build', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').scripts?.build));
88	  it('has scripts.start', () => assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').scripts?.start));
89	  it('devDependencies includes concurrently', () =>
90	    assert.ok(readPkg('package.json').devDependencies?.concurrently));
91	});
92	
93	// ── Subtask 3: Directory structure ───────────────────────────────────────────
94	describe('Directory structure', () => {
95	  const dirs = [
96	    'client',
97	    'client/src',
98	    'server',
99	    'server/db',
100	    'server/api',
101	    'server/ws',
102	  ];
103	  for (const dir of dirs) {
104	    it(`directory exists: ${dir}`, () =>
105	      assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, dir)), `Missing: kanban/${dir}/`));
106	  }
107	});
108	
109	// ── Subtask 4: Workspace package.json files ──────────────────────────────────
110	describe('Client package.json', () => {
111	  it('file exists', () => assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, 'client/package.json'))));
112	  it('name is "kanban-client"', () => assert.equal(readPkg('client/package.json').name, 'kanban-client'));
113	  it('has scripts.dev',     () => assert.ok(readPkg('client/package.json').scripts?.dev));
114	  it('has scripts.build',   () => assert.ok(readPkg('client/package.json').scripts?.build));
115	  it('has scripts.preview', () => assert.ok(readPkg('client/package.json').scripts?.preview));
116	});
117	
118	describe('Server package.json', () => {
119	  it('file exists', () => assert.ok(existsSync(resolve(ROOT, 'server/package.json'))));
120	  it('name is "kanban-server"', () => assert.equal(readPkg('server/package.json').name, 'kanban-server'));
121	  it('has scripts.dev',   () => assert.ok(readPkg('server/package.json').scripts?.dev));
122	  it('has scripts.start', () => assert.ok(readPkg('server/package.json').scripts?.start));
123	});
124	```
125	
126	**How to run the tests** (works without installing anything because Node 20 ships `node:test`):
127	```bash
128	cd kanban-board-1
129	node --test kanban/test/setup.test.mjs
130	```
131	
132	Expected output at this point: **all tests fail** (files/dirs don't exist).
133	
134	---
135	
136	## Subtask 1 — Root package.json (Green Phase)
137	
138	### File to create: `kanban/package.json`
139	
140	```json
141	{
142	  "name": "kanban-app",
143	  "version": "1.0.0",
144	  "private": true,
145	  "workspaces": ["client", "server"],
146	  "scripts": {
147	    "dev": "concurrently -n server,client -c blue,green \"npm -w server run dev\" \"npm -w client run dev\"",
148	    "build": "npm -w client run build",
149	    "start": "npm -w server run start",
150	    "test:setup": "node --test test/setup.test.mjs"
151	  },
152	  "devDependencies": {
153	    "concurrently": "^8.2.2"
154	  }
155	}
156	```
157	
158	**Key decisions:**
159	- `private: true` prevents accidental publish of the root package
160	- `workspaces: ["client", "server"]` (relative to kanban/) enables `npm -w` targeting
161	- `concurrently` version ^8.2.2 (stable, widely used, supports `-n`/`-c` flags)
162	- Scripts use `npm -w <workspace> run <script>` pattern (standard npm workspaces)
163	- `test:setup` script makes the structural test runnable via npm
164	
165	**After writing this file**, run tests again:
166	- ✅ Subtask 1 tests pass
167	- ✅ Subtask 2 tests pass (all checks are JSON reads; they verify field presence, not runtime install)
168	- ❌ Subtask 3 tests still fail (no directories)
169	- ❌ Subtask 4 tests still fail (no workspace package.json files)
170	
171	---
172	
173	## Subtask 2 — Install concurrently (deferred to end)
174	
175	The JSON fields (`scripts.dev`, `devDependencies.concurrently`) are written as part of
176	Subtask 1 (`kanban/package.json`), so all Subtask 2 tests are green as soon as that
177	file is created.
178	
179	**`npm install` must NOT be run until Subtasks 3 and 4 are complete.** npm workspaces
180	resolves all workspace packages during install — if `kanban/client/package.json` or
181	`kanban/server/package.json` don't exist yet, npm fails with:
182	```
183	npm error Missing local dependency: client
184	```
185	
186	The actual install command is run as the very last step after all files are in place.
187	See **Final Verification** below.
188	
189	---
190
